_comment: Previous-run manifest fixture for diff mode: only the aggregate model exists (same checksum), and the fine-tune + KPI exposures are byte-identical to the current manifest — so a checksum diff selects exactly the two new models and the Salesforce exposure that depends on one of them.

model.acmecloud_analytics.customer_arr_b.name: customer_arr_by_region
model.acmecloud_analytics.customer_arr_b.description: Revenue dashboard aggregates ARR by region.
model.acmecloud_analytics.customer_arr_b.raw_code: SELECT region, SUM(arr) FROM customers GROUP BY region
model.acmecloud_analytics.customer_arr_b.compiled_code: SELECT region, SUM(arr) FROM customers GROUP BY region

exposure.acmecloud_analytics.support_ass.name: support_assistant_finetune
exposure.acmecloud_analytics.support_ass.label: Support assistant fine-tune
exposure.acmecloud_analytics.support_ass.description: Fine-tune a support assistant on raw support ticket text.

metatate.use: fine-tune a support assistant on ticket text

exposure.acmecloud_analytics.weekly_kpi_.name: weekly_kpi_email
exposure.acmecloud_analytics.weekly_kpi_.label: Weekly KPI email
exposure.acmecloud_analytics.weekly_kpi_.description: Un-annotated exposure — the adapter reports it as skipped.
